canonical_definition: A property of the solution landscape where a model's parameters reside in a wide, flat region of the loss function, which is correlated with better generalization.
papers: FlatQuant: Flatness Matters for LLM Quantization
SAFE: Finding Sparse and Flat Minima to Improve Pruning

relationships.paper: SAFE: Finding Sparse and Flat Minima to Improve Pruning
relationships.evidence: We attend to recent studies that have demonstrated that the generalization performance of a model is closely linked to the flatness of the solution landscape (Keskar et al., 2017; Jiang et al., 2020a). (1. Introduction)
